name: Depopulated Otín and Mascún Canyon - circular from Rodellar
name_original: Despoblado de Otín y Cañon del Mascún – circular desde Rodellar
region_intro: Hiking around Bierge offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park in Huesca, Aragon. The region is characterized by deep canyons, gorges, and unique geological formations sculpted by the Alcanadre and Isuala rivers. Hikers will encounter varied terrain, from river valleys to limestone cliffs and dense Mediterranean vegetation. This area is a key gateway to a protected natural area known for its impressive geological features. Best hiking trails around Bierge The most popular hiking route is Depopulated Otín and Mascún Canyon - circular from Rodellar, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route explores the rugged Mascún Canyon and the historic village of Otín. Another top favourite among local hikers is Source of Tamara from the Salto de Bierge, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This trail leads to the Fuente de Tamara, known for its emerald waters, and passes by the Salto de Bierge waterfall. Local hikers also love the Virgen del Castillo Hermitage Loop from Rodellar, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain to the Hermitage of the Virgen del Castillo, often completed in about 3 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around Bierge is defined by deep canyons, river gorges, and unique rock formations like Peña Falconera. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Bierge are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Bierge's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bierge?
A: There are nearly 50 hiking routes around Bierge, offering a wide range of experiences within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. These include 15 easy, 13 moderate, and 20 difficult trails, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Bierge?
A: The Bierge area is renowned for its dramatic geological formations. You'll encounter deep canyons, gorges, and unique rock formations like the Peña Falconera. Key natural features include the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Bierge&lt;/a&gt; waterfall and the emerald waters of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tamara Spring&lt;/a&gt;, both accessible via hiking trails.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes starting from Bierge?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14943309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Bierge – Garganta de Tamara loop from Bierge&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.3 km trail that allows you to experience the waterfall and spring before returning to the village.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Bierge?
A: While many trails involve varied terrain, there are easier options suitable for families. The region has 15 easy routes, which typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances. It's advisable to check individual route details for suitability with younger children.

Q: Can I go wild swimming during a hike in Bierge?
A: Absolutely. The Bierge area is famous for its natural pools and crystal-clear rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Bierge&lt;/a&gt;, a 10-meter waterfall, is a popular spot for wild swimming. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de Tamara&lt;/a&gt; also offers beautiful emerald waters for a refreshing dip.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can be found along the trails?
A: Hikers can discover several historical and cultural points of interest. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2984930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Losa Mora&lt;/a&gt;, a Neolithic burial chamber offering scenic views, and the Hermitage of the Virgen del Castillo, which can be reached via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947046261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen del Castillo Hermitage Loop from Rodellar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Bierge a good destination for birdwatching while hiking?
A: Yes, the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, where Bierge is located, is designated as an Area of Special Protection for Birds (ZEPA). This makes it an excellent spot for birdwatching, particularly for observing vultures and other rupicolous species amidst the dramatic landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bierge?
A: The hiking routes in Bierge are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Nearly 3,000 hikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the stunning canyons, unique rock formations, and the refreshing water features like the Salto de Bierge.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of unique rock formations?
A: Yes, the region is known for its unique geology. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodellar Climbing Area – Rock Arch of Peña Oltá loop from Parking Rodellar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that takes you past the impressive Rock Arch of Peña Oltá, showcasing the area's distinctive rock formations.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Bierge?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally considered the best time for hiking in Bierge. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable for hiking, especially if you plan to enjoy the wild swimming spots.

Q: Are there options for parking near the trailheads in Bierge?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads, especially those starting from Bierge or nearby villages like Rodellar, offer designated parking areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodellar Climbing Area – Rock Arch of Peña Oltá loop&lt;/a&gt; explicitly mentions starting from Parking Rodellar, indicating convenient access.
tips: From this elevated spot you have a good chance of seeing the Pyrenees in the distance. The steep peaks stand out clearly from the surrounding green hills. In the landscape that spreads out before you, there are no signs of human civilization except for a few derelict shacks. The descendants of the last villagers are some large and wild goats, but they always keep a safe distance from the hikers.

The Losa Mora dolmen is located within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara natural park, near Rodellar and the Mascún canyon. This burial chamber dates from the Neolithic and consists of large slabs in a good state of preservation. Being on top of a hill, the visit to the dolmen also allows you to enjoy beautiful views of the mountains.

In the middle of the Sierra de Guara natural park lies the abandoned mountain village of Otín. The small church of the place is still very well preserved. Nevertheless, you must be careful when visiting the village: All buildings are dilapidated and the ceilings collapse. Of course, there is nothing in the way of a break in the shade of the trees overlooking the small houses.

You can let your gaze wander far above the riverbed of the Río Mascún. While vultures circle in the warm updrafts down in the canyon, you can enjoy the view of the surrounding mountains and the rugged rock faces.

waypoints.name: Path to Otín & view of the Pirineos
